stat_of_week: 19 HR - BaseballPro77 set a new single-week team record with 19 home runs, powered by Mike Trout's 6-HR, 10-run, 11-RBI explosion, the most dominant individual offensive performance of the season so far
thriller_note: A dead-even tie that neither team can feel good about — Canberra left wins on the table with only 49.2 IP dragging down their pitching categories, while Riverside's .368 OBP and 8 NSVH weren't enough to pull away against a toothless Wombats offense, leaving both teams stuck in neutral heading into Week 5.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 delivered the most dominant offensive week of the season, shattering the team HR record with 19 home runs and tying the team RBI record with 39, all while posting a 2.33 ERA to roll to a 7–2 blowout of the Wisconsin Rapids Reapers. Mike Trout was an otherworldly force — 10 runs, 6 HR, 11 RBI, and a .486 OBP in a single week — supported by Munetaka Murakami and Austin Riley piling on. This was a statement performance that catapults BaseballPro77 into serious title contention and raises real questions about whether anyone in the league can match their two-way firepower right now.
lucky_reason: Despite posting the league's worst strikeout total (45 K), only 4 HR, and a 4.40 ERA — bottom-tier stats in most pitching and power categories — the Benchwarmers carry a luck delta of +1.21, the highest in the league, meaning their actual wins far exceed what their category performance should realistically have produced.
unlucky_reason: The Twins posted a genuinely strong offensive week — 14 HR, 37 runs, 8 SB, and José Ramírez with a .500 OBP — and still carry the league's worst luck delta at -0.61, meaning their strong category totals are consistently not translating into wins the way the numbers say they should.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 set two new single-week records: most HR by a team with 19 (previous mark was not formally held, establishing the new benchmark) and most RBI by a team with 39, matching and officially setting the season record.
